title: Honey Garlic Baked Chicken and Cauliflower (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: Honey Garlic Baked Chicken and Cauliflower combines tender chicken with roasted cauliflower and a sweet-savory honey garlic flavor. It is built for easy meal prep and fast Eati logging.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Chicken
- Cauliflower
- Garlic
- Honey or sweetener
- Soy sauce
- Herbs
- Spices
- Salt and pepper

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Preheat oven and arrange cauliflower on a baking tray.
2. Make a honey garlic sauce with garlic, soy sauce, sweetener, herbs, and spices.
3. Season chicken and place it on the tray.
4. Bake until the chicken is cooked and cauliflower is tender.
5. Spoon sauce over and finish to taste; serve and log your portion in Eati.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 324 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 10.7g
Protein: 41g

Tip: measure honey/sweetener carefully to keep calories predictable.

sections.heading: Why Honey Garlic Chicken Is an Elite Fat-Loss Meal
sections.content:
At just 324 calories with 41g of protein and 11g of fat, this meal is one of the single most efficient high-protein dinners you can make. The protein-to-calorie ratio puts it in the top 1% of recipes.

What makes it efficient:
• Lean chicken base: 6 oz breast = 275 cal, 42g protein.
• Cauliflower: Just 25 cal per cup raw, adds huge volume.
• Tiny amount of honey: 1 tsp honey per serving = 20 cal.
• Minimal oil: 1/2 tsp per serving = 20 cal.
• Aromatics (garlic, ginger, herbs): near zero calories.

Why protein density matters:
When you're dieting, every meal should 'earn' its calories through either protein (for muscle/satiety) or volume (for fullness). This recipe scores high on both — 41g of muscle-protecting protein in a big plate of food.

Comparison to similar sweet chicken dishes:
• Panda Express Honey Sesame Chicken Breast: 420 cal, 15g protein.
• Panera's Asian Chicken Bowl: 500+ cal, 25g protein.
• This recipe: 324 cal, 41g protein.

The protein is more than double, calories are lower. For more protein-dense options, see [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods). Use a calorie calculator to see how it fits your target.

sections.heading: Honey vs Alternative Sweeteners: Calorie & Flavor Comparison
sections.content:
The honey in honey garlic sauce is a sweetness shortcut, but alternatives exist if you're minimizing sugar:

Per tablespoon comparison:
| Sweetener | Calories | Carbs | Flavor |
|-----------|----------|-------|---------|
| Honey | 64 | 17g | Deep, floral, sticky |
| Maple syrup | 52 | 13g | Warm, complex |
| Agave | 60 | 16g | Neutral sweet |
| Monk fruit | 0 | 0 | Clean sweet (some aftertaste) |
| Allulose | 10 | 2g | Neutral, caramelizes well |
| Brown sugar | 45 | 12g | Molasses notes |

For this recipe:
• Traditional (honey): Best flavor, most authentic. 64 cal per tbsp.
• Lower calorie (maple): 52 cal per tbsp, slightly different flavor.
• Zero calorie (monk fruit/allulose): Saves 50+ cal per serving, slight flavor compromise.
• Hybrid approach (half honey, half monk fruit): Best of both — still tastes like honey garlic but with half the sugar calories.

Using 1 tsp honey per serving (this recipe's amount) is a moderate, fat-loss-friendly use of honey. You get the flavor with minimal calorie impact.

sections.heading: Cauliflower Cooking Techniques for Maximum Flavor
sections.content:
Cauliflower deserves better than boring. Here's how to make it actually delicious in this sheet pan recipe:

The key: high heat + enough space.

Prep technique:
• Cut florets similar sizes (thumb-sized) for even cooking.
• Pat completely dry — water = steam = no browning.
• Toss with 1 tsp oil (minimum needed for browning).
• Season before cooking, not after — seasoning sticks better to oiled surfaces.

Roasting temperatures:
• 425°F: standard recipe heat, 20–25 min.
• 450°F: faster browning, 18–22 min (watch carefully).
• 400°F: more even cook but less caramelization.

Arrangement:
• Single layer on the sheet pan.
• Don't crowd — cauliflower needs space to release moisture.
• Use two sheet pans if needed.

Flavor upgrades:
• Finish with lemon zest or juice.
• Add grated parmesan in the last 5 minutes.
• Toss with fresh herbs right before serving.
• Add red pepper flakes for heat.

Common mistake: Adding sauce before roasting. Sweet sauces like honey garlic burn at high temps. Brush sauce on the cauliflower in the last 5 minutes only — this prevents burning and maintains glaze.

Substitution options:
• Broccoli (slightly shorter cook time, similar macros).
• Brussels sprouts (longer cook time, 25–30 min).
• Mix of cauliflower, broccoli, and carrots for color variety.

sections.heading: Variations and Asian-Inspired Additions
sections.content:
The honey garlic base is versatile. Here are flavor variations:

Honey Sesame version:
• Add 1 tsp sesame oil to sauce.
• Top with toasted sesame seeds.
• Sprinkle with scallions.

Spicy Honey Garlic:
• Add 1–2 tsp sriracha to sauce.
• Stir in 1/4 tsp crushed red pepper.
• Top with thinly sliced jalapeños.

Honey Teriyaki:
• Add 1 tbsp pineapple juice.
• Add 1/2 tsp grated ginger.
• Increase soy sauce slightly.

Korean-inspired:
• Add 1 tsp gochujang (Korean chili paste) to the sauce.
• Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and scallions.
• Serve over cauliflower rice.

General Tso-style:
• Add 1 tbsp rice vinegar and 1 tsp hoisin.
• Serve with cauliflower rice or brown rice (add 100 cal).

Serving suggestions:
• Over cauliflower rice for low-carb (~35 cal per cup).
• Over 1/2 cup brown rice for balanced (~110 cal).
• Over rice noodles (not traditional but delicious, ~200 cal per cup).
• In lettuce wraps for a handheld option.
• Top with toasted sesame seeds and scallions.

Link to related recipes: [30 best high-protein recipes](/blog/30-best-high-protein-recipes) and [sheet pan chicken and cauliflower](/blog/sheet-pan-chicken-and-cauliflower-recipe).

sections.heading: Meal Prep & Storage
sections.content:
Honey garlic chicken and cauliflower is a meal-prep favorite.

Batch cooking:
• Double the recipe using two sheet pans.
• Rotate pans halfway through for even browning.
• Total cook time: 25 min regardless of batch size.

Storage:
• Fridge: 3–4 days in airtight containers.
• Freezer: 2 months for chicken alone; cauliflower doesn't freeze well (gets mushy).

Reheating:
• Oven (best for cauliflower texture): 375°F for 10 minutes.
• Skillet: Medium heat, 4–5 minutes with 1 tsp water added.
• Microwave: 90 seconds covered. Cauliflower loses some texture but flavor remains.

Meal prep pro tip: Keep cauliflower and chicken in separate compartments. Pour a little extra honey garlic sauce into a small container and mix at serving time. This prevents the cauliflower from getting soggy.

Leftover ideas:
• Chop into a grain bowl with rice and extra vegetables.
• Stuff into lettuce wraps with cucumber and carrots.
• Add to ramen or a simple noodle bowl.
• Top a salad with cold chicken and honey garlic as dressing.

For tracking accuracy across variations, read [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly).

conclusion: A sweet-savory high-protein dinner that stays simple. Use Eati to portion it and keep macros on track.
metaTitle: Honey Garlic Baked Chicken & Cauliflower: 41g Protein in 324 Calories
metaDescription: Low-calorie honey garlic chicken and cauliflower with 41g of protein in just 324 calories per serving. Sweetener alternatives, cauliflower roasting techniques, and 5 flavor variations.
targetKeyword: honey garlic baked chicken cauliflower high protein

faqs.question: How many calories in honey garlic baked chicken and cauliflower?
faqs.answer: Approximately 324 calories per serving with 41g of protein and 11g of fat. Using monk fruit instead of honey saves another 20 calories per serving. This is one of the most protein-efficient recipes (over 12g of protein per 100 calories).

faqs.question: Can I use a sugar-free sweetener instead of honey?
faqs.answer: Yes. Monk fruit, allulose, and erythritol blends all work. Use 1 tsp sugar-free sweetener for each 1 tsp of honey the recipe calls for. Allulose is best for caramelization; monk fruit for cleanest flavor. This saves 20+ calories per serving.

faqs.question: How do I keep cauliflower from getting soggy?
faqs.answer: Four keys: (1) Pat cauliflower completely dry before seasoning. (2) Roast at high temperature (425°F or higher). (3) Don't overcrowd the sheet pan — use two pans if needed. (4) Don't add sauce until the last 5 minutes to prevent burning and sogginess.

faqs.question: Can I make honey garlic chicken in the Instant Pot?
faqs.answer: Yes, but you lose the caramelized texture. Sauté mode to brown chicken, then pressure cook 8 min. Add cauliflower after release and sauté 3 more minutes. Oven method is better for flavor; Instant Pot saves time.

faqs.question: Is honey garlic chicken keto-friendly?
faqs.answer: As written, not quite — the honey adds about 5g carbs per serving. For keto, substitute monk fruit or allulose for the honey. The resulting dish is fully keto-compliant (under 7g net carbs per serving with cauliflower).
